raw_text.question: Order the following tasks in terms of the waterfall model: acceptance testing, project planning, unit testing, requirements review, cost estimating, high-level design, market analysis, low-level design, systems testing, design review, implementation, requirement specification.
raw_text.response: Okay, now I have to take some tasks and order them in terms of the waterfall model. I know what that is. Waterfall model is about developing software, but not iteratively. So just doing all of the first step, you know, like planning, and then more low-level planning, and so on, and so on, and so on, and only do the testing part. afterwards with no iterative kind of development. Let's just list all of them out on paper. I have acceptance testing. That's in the testing, so it will be late. Project planning, that seems like beginning. Unit testing seems like something that is a smaller test, so it will come before acceptance testing. Requirements review. I think we need to know the requirements before the planning, because it's wonderful, so that I'll put this before planning. Work review. Cost estimation, well, you need to know the requirements to do that, but you do planning after you know the cost so that your plan would take that into consideration, that's cost estimation. High level design, something that would come after planning. Market analysis is in the beginning before even you need to know if people need your product before you start to kind of see what the requirements are. So that's market analysis. Low level design would be after high level design because you design to the code like that. System testing would be probably after acceptance testing. I have to check that because I'm not sure what acceptance testing is. Design review, that's after design, before implementation. So that would be after low-level design, that's design review, then implementation between design review and unit testing. Requirement specification, what was the other one? Requirement review, where you have to specify and then review. So rec... REC specification. I have market analysis, requirement specification, requirement review, cost estimate, planning, high-level design, dollar design, design review, implementation testing, accepting testing, and systems testing. I think systems should be latest because it's the... But let's see what accepting testing is in Waterfall. Let's Google that. Settings testing... Acceptance testing is a formal process where users evaluate the system. Of course, you would evaluate it after it passes integration and system test, so that would be last. Let's switch out acceptance testing and system testing. Yes, this is indeed a stupid methodology.
